summary: External review cycle #1 (sourcery-ai[bot]) addressed: toDelete and toCheck slices in finalizeDatabaseResources were consolidated into a single mariaDBResourceCtors slice of constructor functions, eliminating the drift risk when MariaDB resource types are added or removed. Both Delete and Get phases now iterate over the same shared slice. The refactor is minimal, functionally equivalent, and preserves all existing behavior (NotFound tolerance, idempotency, brownfield no-op). Unit tests for finalizeDatabaseResources and TestReconcile_Terminating* still pass; go vet and golangci-lint are clean for the changed file.

tests_checklist.item: Existing finalizer unit tests still pass after refactor
Edge cases covered (NotFound, brownfield no-op, idempotent re-run)
No new tests required — refactor preserves public contract

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns in reconcile_database.go
Single source of truth eliminates duplication (DRY)
Comment explains WHY the centralization exists (drift prevention)
Feature ID (CC-0078) retained in comment block

security_checklist.item: No new user inputs introduced by refactor

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — constructor-slice pattern is minimal
No premature abstraction (only 3 resource types today, but drift risk is real)
Preserves the finalizer contract (done=true only when all three NotFound)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Parallel resource lists collapsed into a single slice
No speculative abstraction beyond what the drift-fix requires

fail_fast_checklist.item: Delete errors still surface immediately with resource type context
Get errors still surface immediately with resource type context

defensive_checklist.item: NotFound still tolerated per-resource on both Delete and Get
SetName/SetNamespace used via client.Object interface — safe for metav1 objects

next_steps: Proceed to merge — external reviewer feedback addressed, no new issues introduced.
